feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Integrating SAP BTP Cockpit with SSO
entry.content:
We have the desire to integrate our SAML Identity Provider to the login of SAP BTP Cockpit.

We wanted to know if this is possible, because we couldn't find any configuration related to the login parameters of SAP BTP Cockpit.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Unable to subscribe SAP Build Process Automation in trial account.
entry.content: I am unable to subscribe SAP Build Process Automation in my trial account. Checked with the entitlements, it is still showing subscription failed.

entry.title: SAP Build Workzone Subscription Failed
entry.content:
HI  Experts, I am using my Trial Account in SAP BTP, When I'm trying to create a instance/subscription for SAP Build Workzone , I'm getting error as such

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Getting error as such 'Service broker error: Service broker portal responded with invalid JSON: 404 Not Found: Requested route ('portal-service.cert.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com') does not exist.' I have installed the Cloud Identity Services also, but still facing this issue.Kindly Help

entry.title: Integration suite is Unsubscription failed showing in trail account.
entry.content:
I have created a trial account and subscribed to the integration suite service. The Integration suite could be showing unsubscription failed. And not open.

Couldn't unsubscribe from Integration Suite. Please try again. If the problem persists, post a question in the SAP Community.

I tried it several times yesterday and today.

Is there any idea to resolve the problem.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: My Subscription for SAP Build Process Automation in sap btp cockpit free trial account is failing it says subscription failed please contact sap community i followed https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-subscribe-booster.html

entry.title: Is there an API to check the list of roles associated with role collections in BTP?
entry.content:
We are using User Management (System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM)) to check and manage role information.

In Buildworkzone, we were able to confirm user associations with roles using an API.

Is it also possible to check the list of roles associated with a role collection  an API?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Connect 2 SAP BTP accounts
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I would like to check if it is possible to connect 2 SAP BTP account to transfer information from one to another one (using the SAP BTP APIs enabled for that, for sharing information about the security settings or the subaccounts). If it is possible, how should I perform this connection?

Thank you!

Regards.

entry.title: can we use a Jenkinsfile when using sap btp ci/cd
entry.content:
hi !

using ci/cd of sap btp, it uses ".pipeline/config.yml" of root repository but we want to customize a pipeline and then can we use "Jenkinsfile" file for configuring custom pipeline.

thanks in advance.

entry.title: userReferences information with GET API of Authorization/Application does not returned
entry.content:
Hello Experts

/sap/rest/authorization/v2/rolecollecations/roles/{appId}/{roleTemplateName}/{roleName}
I tried to refer to userReferences information with the API.
RoleReferences were returned, but userReferences were not returned.
Do I need to set up a BTP environment to check userReferences?
Could you please how to know to check the information in userReferences?

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Subscribing Failed in Singapore Region Subaccount
entry.content:
Hi BTP Guru,

I am not able to subscribe SAP Build Process Automation in Singapore Region BTP .

entry.title: HANA Active Active read-only
entry.content: If we have setup Active/Active (Read Enabled) to enable read access to the secondary system using HSR and used a hint in ABAP as per  2731728 - Using SAP HANA Active/Active out of customer ABAP programs. How can we check that the query in question has indeed run on Secondary and not on primary. I can check expensive SQL statements/statements executed directly via HANA Studio/HANA cockpit to know that the query in question has indeed run on secondary because secondary system designated to be read-only can be logged onto and run monitoring queries but how can we check this from ABAP. Is there a way of knowing which site it executed on from ABAP layer

entry.title: BTP Core API Entitlement not giving proper response and no api for fetching subscription of SA
entry.content:
Hello Experts ,

i am using BTP core Entitlement API to fetch the entitlements of the subaccount and global account but the api are not returning the entitlement which are assigned to subaccount it is returning all the details and same is with the case of global account also , what i am missing here i am sending the request as per the documentation and created cloud management service central plan and triggering request from postman and all the below api has same results, also i cloud not found any api for gating active subscription of the subaccount ,please suggest all endpoints mentioned below returning same data:

1- Entitlements Service - /v1/assignments/?subaccountGUID=<saguid> &includeAutoManagedPlans=false&entitledServicesOnly=true

2- Entitlements Service - /v1/globalAccountAssignments

3- Entitlements Service - /v1/globalAccountAssignments/?subaccountGUID=<saguid> &includeAutoManagedPlans=false&entitledServicesOnly=true

Thanks,

Siddharth

SAP Business Technology Platform API

entry.title: Challenge with SAP Event Mesh: Status Code 200 in Postman and ABAP, but 503 in Event Mesh
entry.content:
Problem Statement:

In our current architecture, we use SAP Event Mesh to transmit messages to SAP IS-U (R3). The process involves an SAP Event Mesh webhook calling a SICF service under the path /sap/event_mesh_api/ via the URL /sap/event_mesh_api/events?sap-client=XXX within our IS-U environment. However, in tests with Postman and an ABAP report, we get different results: 

Test with Postman and ABAP Report: 

When testing the target URL /sap/event_mesh_api/events?sap-client=XXX using Postman or an ABAP report, we receive an HTTP status code 200, indicating a successful connection and response. 

 

Test with SAP Event Mesh Webhook: 

However, when the Event Mesh webhook calls the same URL, an HTTP status code 503 is logged in Event Mesh. This suggests that the target URL is either unreachable or there is a failure in server-side processing. 

 

Monitoring and Troubleshooting: 

No messages are displayed in Cloud Connector Monitoring. This could mean that the message either never reaches the Cloud Connector or that an error in processing prevents the message from being correctly logged. 

 

Internal Test Service: 

To further test the functionality of Event Mesh, we connected an internal test service. When triggering the service through the webhook, we observed that the messages were successfully delivered via Event Mesh to the service. This confirms that the basic configuration of Event Mesh is working. 

 

Issue with the Cloud Connector: 

However, a problem arises when the Cloud Connector is involved in the communication. The messages that are supposed to be sent via the Event Mesh webhook to the IS-U environment do not reach the Cloud Connector. We suspect that the issue lies within the Cloud Connector configuration, but we have not yet identified the exact cause. 

 

Contact with SAP Support: 

SAP Support was unable to reproduce the problem in their environment; the connection worked flawlessly on their side. 

Question to the Community: We are facing the challenge that the Event Mesh webhook does not provide the same response as the tests in Postman or ABAP. Instead, it returns a status code 503, indicating a service failure. 

Has anyone in the community experienced similar issues with SAP Event Mesh? If so, what steps or solutions were helpful in troubleshooting? 

Some specific questions where we need assistance: 

Different Status Codes: Why might the Event Mesh webhook return a status code 503, while direct tests with Postman or ABAP return a status code 200? 

 

Monitoring Issue: Why do the messages not appear in Cloud Connector Monitoring? Are there specific settings or logs that could provide more insight into the cause of the problem? 

 

Cloud Connector Issue: Has anyone experienced similar issues related to Cloud Connector configuration? What specific points should we check? 

 

Additional Tests or Configurations: What other tests or configuration changes could be helpful in identifying the root cause of the problem? 

We look forward to your experiences, tips, and solutions to help resolve this issue. 

Thank you in advance for your support! 

Attachment: Following this post, you will find screenshots documenting the issues described, configuration and our previous tests. 

 

 

 

 

 

 


#SAPEventMesh #SAPBTP #CloudConnector #SAPIntegration #ISU #ABAP #SAPTechWebhooks #SAPSupport #SAPArchitectur

entry.title: Call Unbound Action of a CAP remote Service from Action of a CAP Service
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to call an unbound action of a CAP Service from another CAP Service. When calling the action of the remote i am getting the below error. 

Steps performed

Create and deploy a CAP Service with an unbound action accepting 2 parameters.

Import the EDMX of the service into new CAP Application.

Add a New Bound Action in the new CAP Project, Call Action of the remote service as per the below documentation

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#calling-actions-functions

Error :

this.__proto__.toJSON is not a function","stacktrace":["TypeError: this.__proto__.toJSON is not a function","at e.toJSON (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:209:32)","at JSON.stringify (<anonymous>)","at Object.format (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/log/format/json.js:105:18)","at fmt (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/log/cds-log.js:93:38)","at logger.warn (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/log/cds-log.js:100:68)","at run (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:233:22)","at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)","at async RemoteService.on_handler (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:265:20)","at async next (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:68:17)","at async RemoteService.handle

entry.title: Re: Could not get the ApiHub resource data
entry.content:
Hi,

I don't know if you have solved it yet, but I'm having the same issue and I wanted to share a way around this problem.

The solution consists on creating a Destination in SAP BTP and consuming the Business Accelerator Hub API through it in BAS.

1. Access Business Accelerator Hub and search for the API you want to consume. In my case, I'm using Sales Order (A2X) as a demo.

2. Once in the API page, scroll down in the Overview tab to the API resources. There, click on Configuration Details. We will be using the links provided there. In my case, I'll copy the sandbox URL.

3. Go to the Destination section of your SAP BTP subaccount. Here you have to creat a Destination for Business Accelerator Hub with the following configuration.

The property URL.headers.ApiKey will not appear as an option in the dropdown, you have to write it yourself. The value will be your Business Accelerator Hub Api key.

The URL will be "https://sandbox.api.sap.com" for sandbox.

4. We can now go to BAS and add the service in the Service Center. Instead of selecting the Business Accelerator Hub in the Provider selection, we will choose 'SAP SYSTEM'. There, our recently created destination will appear. If it doesn't show, refresh BAS.

 5. We select the apihub_sandbox service. In the Service Path Input, we have to copy the half of the API URL that we left behind when we created the Destination.

  • The part we used for the destination: https://sandbox.api.sap.com 
  • Our API URL: /s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_salesorder/srvd_a2x/sap/salesorder/0001

After clicking on Connect, the Add to Project button should be displayed and the API content should appear below the Connect button. After that, we can add the service to our project.

 I hope that you find this useful.

Kind Regards

entry.title: How to get the HTML code of a website with SAP Build Process Automation?
entry.content: Hallo zusammen, ich versuche einen Prozess zu erstellen, um die Preise einiger Produkte zu prüfen. Dazu muss ich einige Links auf einer aufgezeichneten Website öffnen und den HTML-Code daraus abrufen. Ich finde in SAP BPA keine Aktivität, um den Code abzurufen. Hat jemand eine Idee, wie ich dieses Problem lösen kann?

entry.title: BPA for ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am looking to create/configure a bot in BPA for SAP Business ByDesign.

This bot has to locate the projects (KUT extension is added to projects of type INDICATOR) that are ready to be assigned with a team on the Header. The team information is available on an EXCEL sheet (placed in an FTP/Sharepoint site).

Can I develop/configure such a bot in BPA to connect to ByDesign, Retrieve the Projects, and then connect to Sharepoint to access the file assign the team, and offset the indicator on the projects automatically in one shot?

I also want to know how to trigger the bot, I mean which of the 3 types would be the appropriate one to trigger the process in the BPA so the bot can do its job?

#SAPBusinessByDesign #ByDesign #BPA #SAPBPA #SAPByD #BTP

entry.title: SAP BTP integration suite trial Account
entry.content:
Hi, SAPIENS

I created a trial account for Integration Suite a few days ago, where I created several flows for my learning purposes. However, when I logged into my account today, it showed a "subscription failed" status. I have attached the error screen for your reference. Can you please help me with this?

entry.title: One of my CDS CQL statements doesn't change the backing postgre-sql database
entry.content:
In my Core Data Services project (running locally but with --hybrid switch to 'connect to' a BTP destination and connected to a postgre-sql database also running locally in a docker container), I have several cql statements, three of which are UPDATE statements.  All but two of the cql statements 'work', including one of the UPDATE statements.  However, two of the UPDATE statements don't seem to update the backing postgre-sql database.

All of the statements are using '.run()' directly on the cds object (none are creating transactions).  My understanding is that cds.run() creates and then (on success) commits a transaction.

Below is the code being run for one of the UPDATE statements that doesn't update postgre-sql.  The two SELECT statements are there to help me diagnose.  When the code runs, the first SELECT shows null for submissionBegunAt.  After the UPDATE runs, the second SELECT shows the date that was assigned during the UPDATE run, for submissionBegunAt.

However, after all the code runs, the database doesn't reflect the changes.

CODE THAT 'DOESN'T WORK'

const markBegunAt = async () => {
let affectedGroup = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
SELECT(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.where(
{
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
}
)
);

console.log(affectedGroup);
//const tx = _closureVariables.cds.transaction();
//_closureVariables.cds
try {
const result = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
UPDATE(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.set({submissionBegunAt: new Date().toISOString()})
.where({
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
})
);

console.log(result);
//await tx.commit();
}
catch(exception) {
console.log(exception);
//await tx.rollback();
}


affectedGroup = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
SELECT(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.where(
{
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
}
)
);

console.log(affectedGroup);

And below is the code for the UPDATE that's 'making it to the backing postgre-sql database'

CODE THAT WORKS

UPDATE(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.set({status: "A"})
.where({
request_formNumber: req.data.requestId,
moduleRole_ID: req.data.roleId,
moduleRole_module_ID: req.data.moduleId
})
 
I don't see any substantive difference in these pieces of code.
 
I appreciate any help!

entry.title: Issue with CF Target not available in BAS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I logged in BAS with CF API endpoint available but still CF target is not available. Please help with the missing part.

 

Regards,Lakshmi
